prompt: Pull the Acumatica project budget for Westfield.

observations: tools fired: backendDeepAgentProjectStatus, project_lookup, packet_reader, teams_reader, meetings_reader, emails_reader, documents_reader, financials_reader, schedule_reader, rfi_reader, submittal_reader, deepagents_runtime
score.finalText:
Direct answer

Westfield Collective’s structured budget is showing as flatlined at $0 across budget, contract, and billing, which is not a real project financial picture — it’s a data-coverage problem, not a clean zero project. Operationally, the project looks quiet in the structured system, but recent email traffic shows closeout/activation issues still hanging around.

Evidence

- Budget summary shows original budget $0, revised budget $0, approved changes $0, and budget delta $0. Source: PM database project budget summary for Westfield Collective.
- Prime contract summary also shows original contract $0, revised contract $0, approved owner COs $0, pending owner COs $0, invoiced $0, paid $0, remaining balance $0. Source: PM database project budget summary for Westfield Collective.
- Project briefing flags no open RFIs, submittals, or overdue schedule tasks in structured tables, and no commitments or change events recorded there. Source: PM database project briefing snapshot for Westfield Collective.
- Recent movement is still active: 2026-06-08 email chain on roll-up counter doors, 2026-06-08 email on roof cost, 2026-06-08 email on duct sweating, and 2026-06-05 email on warranty and O&M manuals. Source: PM database project briefing snapshot for Westfield Collective.
- Document intake includes a contract change order attachment for “Added Electrical for exterior walk in cooler” and a counter shutter drawing file on 2026-06-08. Source: PM database recent documents for Westfield Collective.

What this means

Do not treat the $0 budget as a usable financial read. The structured financials are either not populated or not wired to this project yet, so any margin or exposure conclusion would be fiction. The real signal here is that Westfield is in late-stage closeout/activation mode with active warranty and field coordination items still moving, which means the risk is less “big budget overrun” and more “small unresolved issues turning into payment/admin drag.”

Recommended next steps

- Pull the Acumatica project code mapping for Westfield Collective and verify whether the budget/contract feed is missing or mis-linked.
- Have the PM confirm whether all change work has been entered into the project controls, especially the exterior walk-in cooler electrical change order and any door/shutter scope.
- Before any owner-facing financial update, reconcile recent email/document activity against the budget and committed cost picture.

Uncertainty

- The structured budget and contract fields appear empty or unmapped, so the financial read is incomplete.
- I cannot tell from the current structured data whether the $0 values are real or a sync/mapping issue.

Source coverage note: meetings: missing; rfi: missing; submittal: missing. I did not use unavailable or stale source categories as factual support.

sources.title: RE: Alleato Group: Westfield Collective: Roll Up Counter Doors
sources.snippet:
Subject: RE: Alleato Group: Westfield Collective: Roll Up Counter Doors
Date: 2026-06-08T12:18:19Z
From: James Wallace <james@midwestgaragedoors.com>
To: Andrew Cannon <acannon@alleatogroup.com>

Andrew The rolling door manufacturers have a way of dragging things out so I would not count on them getting back with me today. I will let you know as soon as I hear something.       James Wallace Midwest Garage Doors 437 east stop 18 road Greenwood, Indiana 46143 1-317-889-9500   F

sources.title: Re: Alleato Group: Westfield Collective: Roll Up Counter Doors
sources.snippet:
Subject: Re: Alleato Group: Westfield Collective: Roll Up Counter Doors
Date: 2026-06-08T11:56:51Z
From: Andrew Cannon <acannon@alleatogroup.com>
To: James Wallace <james@midwestgaragedoors.com>

James,  Understood, I will keep my eye out for a call or email. It would be great to have an update back today or tomorrow if possible.  Thank you, Andrew Cannon    Assistant Project Manager at Alleato Group Mobile  317.315.3968 |   Email   acannon@alleatogroup.com Web
